name: Embalse de las Parras — circular desde Utrillas
region_intro: Mountain biking around Utrillas features diverse geological formations, including the Utrillas Formation, which consists of detrital materials like quartz sand, gravel, and conglomerates. This creates varied and often challenging terrain, offering dynamic riding experiences. The region's landscapes provide a mix of sandy and rocky compositions, along with changes in elevation, suitable for mountain biking. These natural features contribute to both challenging climbs and technical descents across the area's established trail networks. Best mountain bike trails around Utrillas The most popular mountain bike trail is Embalse de las Parras — circular desde Utrillas, a 16.9 miles (27.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a scenic circular ride around the reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Viewpoint and Sierra de Sant Just — circular from Montalbán, a difficult 36.5 miles (58.8 km) path. This route provides extensive views and traverses the challenging terrain of the Sierra de Sant Just. Local mountain bikers also love the El Pinaroto y Cima de Majalinos — circular desde El Palomar de Arroyos, a 33.7 miles (54.3 km) trail leading through varied landscapes and towards the Cima de Majalinos, often completed in about 5 hours 35 minutes. Mountain biking around Utrillas is defined by the Utrillas Formation, sandy and rocky terrain, and varied landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Utrillas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Utrillas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Utrillas?
A: Utrillas offers a network of 14 mountain bike trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 10 classified as moderate and 4 as difficult, providing diverse options for riders.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Utrillas?
A: The terrain in Utrillas is shaped by the unique Utrillas Formation, featuring detrital materials like quartz sand, gravel, and conglomerates. This results in varied landscapes with sandy and rocky compositions, along with significant changes in elevation. Riders can expect dynamic experiences, from challenging climbs to technical descents.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Utrillas?
A: While Utrillas offers many moderate and difficult trails, there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' for absolute beginners. However, the 10 moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005569488/embalse-de-las-parras-circular-desde-utrillas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de las Parras — circular desde Utrillas&lt;/a&gt;, are suitable for riders with good physical fitness and some cycling experience.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Utrillas?
A: Yes, Utrillas has 4 difficult mountain bike routes designed for experienced riders. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005500651/viewpoint-and-sierra-de-sant-just-circular-from-montalban" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint and Sierra de Sant Just — circular from Montalbán&lt;/a&gt; offers extensive views and traverses challenging terrain, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005511290/el-pinaroto-y-cima-de-majalinos-circular-desde-el-palomar-de-arroyos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pinaroto y Cima de Majalinos — circular desde El Palomar de Arroyos&lt;/a&gt; leads through varied landscapes with significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Utrillas?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes in Utrillas are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005569488/embalse-de-las-parras-circular-desde-utrillas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de las Parras — circular desde Utrillas&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005500651/viewpoint-and-sierra-de-sant-just-circular-from-montalban" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint and Sierra de Sant Just — circular from Montalbán&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some scenic mountain bike routes with good viewpoints?
A: For scenic views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005500651/viewpoint-and-sierra-de-sant-just-circular-from-montalban" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint and Sierra de Sant Just — circular from Montalbán&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult route provides extensive vistas across the landscape. The region's varied topography, including small anticline structures, naturally offers many elevated points for panoramic views.

Q: Are there any historical or natural landmarks to see along the trails?
A: Yes, the Utrillas area features several points of interest. You might encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5377977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portal de Santa Engracia&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8107834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Hall and Church of the Assumption (Ruins)&lt;/a&gt;. Natural features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/756446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Just Pass (1408 m)&lt;/a&gt;, offering elevated views.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Utrillas?
A: The mountain bike trails in Utrillas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 50 mountain bikers have explored the area, often praising the varied terrain and the dynamic riding experiences offered by the region's unique geological features.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trailheads in Utrillas?
A: While specific parking facilities are not detailed for every trailhead, Utrillas is a municipality with established infrastructure. It's generally possible to find parking in or near the towns where routes begin, such as Utrillas itself, Montalbán, or El Palomar de Arroyos, which serve as starting points for many popular trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Utrillas?
A: Given Utrillas's location in Teruel, Spain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder weather, though the specific impact on trail conditions would depend on local snowfall or rainfall.

Q: Are permits required for mountain biking in Utrillas?
A: There is no indication that specific permits are required for mountain biking on the general trails around Utrillas. However, it's always advisable to respect local regulations and private property signs, especially when venturing into less-trafficked areas.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails in Utrillas family-friendly?
A: The mountain bike trails in Utrillas are predominantly classified as moderate to difficult. While some families with experienced teenage riders might find suitable options, the lack of 'easy' trails means that most routes may be too challenging for younger children or families seeking very gentle rides.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Utrillas?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on many outdoor trails in Spain, especially in natural areas. However, it's crucial to keep dogs under control, preferably on a leash, to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always check for specific local signage or regulations at the trailhead.
tips: Beautiful views of the Cueva de la Hiedra from near the town of Utrillas.

To get to the dam of the Las Parras reservoir we will ride along a smooth path that runs under a poplar. Nearby is the impressive Pajazo waterfall and the Las Palomas waterfall. We will find information panels throughout the area that will give us more information about the place. We remind you that bathing is prohibited in the reservoir
During the way near the town of Utrillas, we find several rafts near the trail that make it much more picturesque and entertaining.
Beautiful views of the Turolense town of Utrillas that will surely make us take out our camera to photograph such a picture.

waypoints.name: View of the Cueva de la Hiedra
